key_insight: Randomized Hamiltonian Monte Carlo mixes faster by randomizing integration times, achieving accelerated KL convergence for log-concave targets and even stronger total-time scaling with a schedule of increasing means.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical sampling strategy for large-scale probabilistic inference and uncertainty quantification when fast, reliable draws from log-concave posteriors are needed.
method: Simulate continuous-time Hamiltonian dynamics with random integration times instead of fixed trajectories.
Reset velocity to an independent Gaussian between segments.
Use triangular or exponential time distributions with mean Θ(α^{-1/2}) to get exponential KL decay; use triangular times with increasing means for O(ε^{-1/2}) total time under general log-concavity.
evidence_signal: The paper provides theoretical convergence guarantees in KL divergence and total integration-time complexity bounds for log-concave distributions, including α-Talagrand and α-strongly log-concave cases.
watch_item: The guarantees depend on log-concavity and on carefully chosen random-time distributions; performance and tuning may be sensitive outside these assumptions.
paper_title: Accelerated Mixing Time of Randomized Hamiltonian Monte Carlo
